actionEn: The road curves sharply to the right — reduce speed in advance, don't run wide on the bend.
The road curves sharply to the left — reduce speed in advance and stay close to the right edge.

nameEn: Dangerous bends — first to the right
actionEn: A series of bends begins, the first to the right — reduce speed and stay alert for the whole section.

nameEn: Dangerous bends — first to the left
actionEn: A series of bends begins, the first to the left — reduce speed and stay alert for the whole section.
An intersection with no traffic lights or priority signs — yield to traffic from the right.

nameEn: Intersection with minor roads on both sides
actionEn: Minor roads join from both sides — your road has priority, but stay alert.

nameEn: Intersection with a minor road on the right
actionEn: A minor road joins from the right — your road has priority, but watch for merging traffic.

nameEn: Intersection with a minor road on the left
actionEn: A minor road joins from the left — your road has priority, but stay alert.

nameEn: One-way road joining from the right
actionEn: A one-way road joins from the right — remember that traffic exiting it only moves in one direction.

nameEn: One-way road joining from the left
actionEn: A one-way road joins from the left — remember that traffic exiting it only moves in one direction.
At the intersection, yield to all traffic on the crossing road; stop if necessary.
A roundabout begins ahead — yield to traffic already on the roundabout, reduce speed.
A crossing with barriers is approaching — reduce speed, watch the barrier and light signal.
A crossing without barriers — come to a full stop, make sure no train is coming from either direction, then proceed.
Uneven road surface (potholes, bumps) — reduce speed.
A speed bump (traffic-calming hump) is ahead — reduce speed significantly.
The road narrows on both sides — reduce speed and yield to oncoming traffic.
The road narrows on the right — watch for traffic merging left.
The road narrows on the left — watch for traffic merging right.
A drawbridge is ahead — considering it may be closed, reduce speed and watch for the signal.
Road works are underway — reduce speed, be careful of workers and equipment.
The road surface may be slippery — reduce speed, avoid sudden braking or turning.
A pedestrian crossing is approaching — reduce speed, be ready to give way to pedestrians.
A school or children's facility is nearby — reduce speed significantly, children may step onto the road unexpectedly.
Domestic animals may enter the road — reduce speed, be careful.
Wild animals may enter the road — reduce speed, especially at night.
A section with possible strong side winds — hold the wheel firmly, reduce speed.
The one-way road ends and two-way traffic begins — keep to your own lane.
The road crosses tram tracks — yield to the tram, watch for wheel slippage on the rails.
A steep descent begins — reduce speed in advance, use a lower gear.
A steep ascent begins — choose the right speed and gear in advance.
A place frequently used by cyclists — reduce speed, leave enough distance.
Rocks or debris may fall onto the road — reduce speed, watch the roadside.
An airport is nearby, low-flying aircraft may be distracting — be careful.
The road ends at a body of water or bank — reduce speed, be ready to stop.
Loose gravel on the road — avoid stone spray and skidding, increase following distance.
Traffic lights ahead that may be hard to see — reduce speed, be ready for the signal.
Another type of danger with no dedicated sign (explained on a supplementary plate) — be careful.
A dangerous roadside (drop-off, ditch) — avoid driving close to the edge.
The road surface may ice over (bridge, shaded area) — reduce speed significantly.
A traffic jam may be ahead — reduce speed, warn following traffic with hazard lights.
An accident scene is ahead — reduce speed, proceed with extra caution.
